Title: A Novel ZjbZIP33-ZjPRX1 Module Positively Regulates Lignin Formation in the Jujube Fruit Stone

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class III peroxidase; conserved peroxidase domain; haem-binding, active, substrate-binding and calcium-binding sites
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses the final oxidative polymerization of lignin monomers to form lignin during endocarp and cell-wall lignification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key enzyme in the last step of lignin synthesis; rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in fruit; strongest in endocarp; higher in 'Jinsi' than 'Wuhe'; abundant at lignifying stages and positively correlated with lignin content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In jujube, enriched in endocarp and fruit tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA treatment; GA3 treatment; CCC treatment; developmental stage of young fruit/endocarp hardening
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit stone hardening, secondary cell wall thickening, seed protection, and influences fruit quality
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit endocarp (stone); also stem secondary cell wall in transgenic Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively promotes lignin accumulation and cell wall thickening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; RNA in situ hybridisation; transient overexpression in jujube fruit; stable overexpression in jujube callus; TRV-based silencing in jujube seedlings; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; lignin quantification; SEM
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of endocarp from 'Jinsi' and 'Wuhe' at S2 and S4; WGCNA based on RNA-seq; Data availability stated as Supporting Information, no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused dwarf phenotype in jujube seedlings; overexpression increased growth and stem thickening in Arabidopsis, implying pleiotropic developmental effects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway gene for modulating stone/endocarp lignification in jujube and potentially breeding softer- or harder-stone fruit types
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected from transcriptome because PRX genes showed the most significant changes among lignin-related genes; function validated in both homologous and heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZjPRX1 expression is strongly enriched in jujube fruit endocarp and is higher in the hard-stone cultivar 'Jinsi' than in stoneless 'Wuhe'. 2. Overexpression in jujube fruits and calli increases lignin accumulation, whereas silencing reduces lignin and causes dwarfism. 3. Heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis increases lignin deposition and stem secondary cell wall thickness. 4. ZjPRX1 is transcriptionally activated by ZjbZIP33 and induced by IAA/GA3, especially GA3.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in fruit; higher in 'Jinsi' endocarp-related datasets than in 'Wuhe'
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In jujube, highly expressed in fruit and associated with endocarp lignification
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ZjPRX1 transcription by binding the G-box element in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lignin biosynthesis / endocarp lignification / secondary cell wall thickening
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as an upstream activator of ZjPRX1 and a positive regulator of lignin formation in jujube fruit stone
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: IAA treatment; GA3 treatment; CCC treatment; developmental stage of young fruit/endocarp hardening
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes fruit stone hardening, secondary cell wall thickening, seed protection, and influences fruit quality
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit endocarp (stone); stem secondary cell walls in transgenic Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates lignin accumulation and cell wall thickening by activating ZjPRX1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA-guided candidate screening; transcriptional activation assay in yeast; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; transient overexpression in jujube fruit; stable overexpression in jujube callus; TRV-based silencing in jujube seedlings; heterologous overexpression in Arabidopsis; lignin quantification; SEM
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of endocarp from 'Jinsi' and 'Wuhe'; WGCNA coexpression analysis; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; no accession provided
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused dwarf phenotype and thinner cell walls in jujube seedlings, indicating broader developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target for engineering or breeding altered stone lignification; can be combined with ZjPRX1 for module-based control of endocarp hardness; caution due to growth effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shown to have transcriptional activation activity in yeast; GA3 had a stronger inductive effect than IAA on the ZjbZIP33-ZjPRX1 module and lignin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZjbZIP33 was identified by promoter cis-element analysis and WGCNA as a likely upstream regulator of ZjPRX1. 2. It is a nucleus-localized bZIP transcriptional activator that directly binds the G-box in the ZjPRX1 promoter. 3. Overexpression increases lignin accumulation in jujube fruits, calli and Arabidopsis, while silencing reduces lignin and causes dwarfism. 4. IAA and especially GA3 induce ZjbZIP33 expression, which in turn activates ZjPRX1 and promotes endocarp lignification.
